revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Hosshinmon-ōji]][[ja:発心門王子]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:HOSSHINMON-oji.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tanabe|qid=Q304266|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}}|part_of={{ill|Gotai-ōji|qid=Q3111472|1=Gotai-ōji}}; {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|qid=Q3200280|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines}}}}
{{infobox ancient site |image = Sacred arch, HOSSHINMON-oji.JPG |caption = Hosshinmon-oji. Looking at the shrine building through the torii gate. }}
{{ill|Hosshinmon Ōji|ja|発心門王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosshinmon Ōji|1=Hosshinmon Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3141012}} (発心門王子) is the site of a former shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|qid=Q304266|lt=Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It was one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, specifically one of the {{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji#五体王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}}. It is part of the '''Kumano Kodo Pilgrimage Routes''', which were designated as a '''National Historic Site of Japan''' on November 2, 2000.<ref name="Tanabe">{{Cite web|和書 |author=Tanabe City Board of Education |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City’s Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}</ref> It was also designated as a '''Prefectural Historic Site''' in 1967, along with the site of '''Nambōdō'''.<ref name="Tanabe"/> == Overview == [[File:HOSSHINMON-oji.JPG|thumb|Shaden]] The pilgrimage path follows the '''Mitsue Pass''' ({{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Nakahechi Town)|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe, Nakahechi)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (田辺市中辺路町)#三越峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Nakahechi Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}}) along the source of the '''Otonashi River''', passing by '''Inohana Ōji''' ({{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe, Hongū)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)#猪鼻王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe, Hongū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}) before ascending into the mountains. Climbing a '''fern-covered slope''', pilgrims enter a sacred zone marked by a '''torii gate''', beyond which lies '''Hosshinmon Ōji'''.
=== Meaning of "Hosshinmon" === The term '''Hosshinmon''' (発心門) originates from '''mountain ascetic training (Shimon Shugyō, 四門修行)'''. In this practice, four gates symbolize spiritual progress: * '''Hosshin (発心)''' – Awakening of the Bodhi Mind (aspiration to enlightenment). * '''Shugyō (修行)''' – Practice and discipline. * '''Tōkaku (等覚)''' – Near enlightenment. * '''Myōkaku (妙覚)''' – Supreme enlightenment.
Thus, '''Hosshinmon''' signifies the '''entrance to the sacred realm'''.<ref name="Kumanoji_1973_132">Kumanoji Compilation Committee [1973: 132]</ref>
A record from '''1109''' ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|qid=Q1193599|lt=Ten'nin|1=Tennin (era)}} 2) describes a '''grand torii gate''' at this location, where pilgrims performed purification rituals before passing through.<ref name="Heibon">Heibonsha [1997: 740]</ref> While '''Hosshinmon''' originally referred to a '''roofed gate''', in this case, it became associated with a torii, leading to the name '''Hosshinmon Ōji'''.
=== Account in Ken'nin Pilgrimage Record === [[File:HOSSHINMON-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|Monument]] The '''Ken'nin Pilgrimage Record''' ({{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|qid=Q1195433|lt=Ken'nin|1=Kennin}} 1, 1201) by '''Fujiwara no Teika''' provides a vivid description of '''Hosshinmon Ōji'''. His account describes the shrine as so '''awe-inspiring''' that it stirred deep faith, with '''lush trees surrounding the shrine''', their '''autumn leaves falling gracefully''' in the wind.<ref name="Heibon"/>
=== From the Mi11le Ages to the Modern Period === While '''Hosshinmon Ōji''' was first recorded in the '''early 12th century''', it only became recognized as one of the '''Five Great Ōji (Gotai Ōji)''' around the '''14th century''', appearing in the '''1326 Kumano Engi (熊野縁起, Jinwa-ji Temple edition).'''<ref name="Heibon"/>
However, as the '''Kumano pilgrimage declined''', the shrine also fell into disrepair. The '''Genroku period''' (1688–1703) records '''only the remnants of the gate''', with a claim that all '''four gates once stood together'''—though no other historical records support this claim.<ref name="Kumanoji_1973_132"/>
By the '''late Edo period''', the '''Kii Zokufudoki (紀伊続風土記, 1806–1839)''' states that the shrine had already '''fallen into ruin''', but was '''rebuilt under orders from the Kishu Domain during the Kyōhō era (1716–1736)''' as a '''branch shrine of Kumano Hongū Taisha'''.<ref name="Heibon"/> However, by this time, the '''main pilgrimage route had already shifted''' to the '''Akagigoe Pass (赤木越)''' leading directly from '''Yunomine Onsen'''.<ref name="Nishi">Nishi [1987: 162]</ref>
In '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', the shrine was '''officially abolished and merged into Sanri Shrine (三里神社) in Hongū Town'''. The original '''shrine building was relocated''', but records confirm its existence at the time.<ref name="Nishi"/> A '''stone monument''' marking the former shrine site was erected and remains today. The '''current shrine structure''' was rebuilt in '''1990 (Heisei 2).'''
== Site of Nambōdō == '''Nambōdō (南無房堂, also called Nambōtaku)''' was a '''Buddhist nunnery''' located near '''Hosshinmon Ōji'''. '''Fujiwara no Teika''' stayed here during his pilgrimage and expressed '''relief at finding decent lodging''', as earlier lodgings had been inadequate.
At the time, it was common for pilgrims to '''leave written inscriptions''' at temples and shrines. Teika followed this custom, writing '''poems on the pillars''' of '''Hosshinmon Ōji''' and '''Nambōdō'''. However, he later learned that the nun of '''Nambōdō''' disliked such '''graffiti''' and felt embarrassed.<ref name="Heibon"/>
Today, only a '''leveled area of land''' remains, marked by an '''informational sign'''.
== Access == - From '''Kii-Tanabe Station''' (JR Kisei Line), take a '''Ryujin Bus''' for '''about 2 hours 30 minutes''' and get off at '''"Hosshinmon Ōji"''' stop. The shrine is '''5–10 minutes on foot'''. - From '''Shingū Station''', take a '''Nara Kotsu, Kumano Gobō Nankai Bus, or Meikō Bus''' for '''1 hour 30 minutes''' to '''"Hongū Taisha-mae"''', then transfer to a '''Ryujin Bus to "Hosshinmon Ōji" (15 minutes).'''
== Location == * '''1652 Kamikubo, Mitsue, Hongū-chō, Tanabe City, Wakayama Prefecture'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book ja-jp|author=Kumanoji Compilation Committee|year=1973|title=Ancient Roads and Ōji Shrines – Kumano Nakahechi|publisher=Kumano Nakahechi Publication Society|series=Kumano Bunko 4}} * {{Cite book ja-jp|author=Nishi Ritsu|year=1987|title=Kumano Kodo Pilgrimage Guide – Investigation of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|publisher=Arao Seibundō|series=Minamoto Library 1}} * {{Cite book ja-jp|editor=Heibonsha|year=1997|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines in Yamato and Kii|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458213402-5}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Nakahechi Route|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi Route|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ''' {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Inohana Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * '''Hosshinmon Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Mizunomi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}'''
